title: THE RESCUE — ECHO LIFELINE PASS V2 + V7
episodeObjective: Reach the unstable emergency pod, keep Maya alive, expose Hale's old pod access, and make the open/abort decision create visible relationship consequences that carry into Episode 10.

summary: Scenario spine preserved: rescue junction, beacon stabilization, route selection, pod exterior, Hale's old access, access protocol, open/abort branch.
All visible choice sets have exactly two options.
CharacterBusy waits reduced to 2–4 minutes for playable real-time pacing.
Return nodes rewritten from clean reports into lived reactions with pauses, corrections, pain, guilt and delayed information.
Maya and Hale visibly remember operator choices inside the episode.
Open/abort decision creates Episode 10 start-state flags and relationship consequences.
UNKNOWN messages are fragmented, urgent and less polished.
Lore is delivered through action, physical evidence, environmental reaction and character conflict.

e09_n01.speaker: MAYA
e09_n01.objective: Set the rescue target and immediate conflict.

events.text: It keeps sliding off the relay map.
Like it doesn't want to be found.
I can see the pulse in the telemetry.
I heard Noah in the last transmission.
Nobody touches that hatch until we know what's moving inside it.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: That's easy for the dead engineer to say.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Things that don't pretend to be dead friends.
Holding position while Hale filters the beacon.
If I move, the beacon starts lying again.

event.speaker: MAYA
event.text: The relay pack is buzzing against my spine.

events.text: At least now it's lying in a straight line.
I hate that I can hear it better now.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Pushing through before the map shifts again.
If this was reckless, remember you picked it.
Pod is closer than the map said.
Beacon dragged across my display while I moved.
Like the pod corrected where I thought it was.
Or the pod moved toward me.

events.title: RESCUE ROUTE MAP
events.content:
ROUTE A: Short route through exposed radiation corridor. Arrival fast. Crew risk severe. Pod risk moderate.

ROUTE B: Long route through maintenance spine. Arrival delayed. Crew risk moderate. Pod risk severe.
events.caption: Recovered rescue route analysis. Both routes carry consequences.
events.message: [RESCUE ROUTE MAP ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: Route A gets me there fast.
If you picked caution before, this is where it gets expensive.
If you like heroic stupidity, Route A is waving at you.
Pick the sin you can live with.
Do not stop in the exposed section.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: I need to run this clean.
Pretty sure I can taste metal.
Didn't know a place could do that.
If my hair falls out, I'm blaming you.
Sensible is a nice word for slow when someone is suffocating.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: I need to crawl most of this.
Don't expect charm when I get back.
Found older boot marks in the dust.
Someone used this route before us.
Both facts are sitting in my chest.
Hull is frosted from the inside.
Noah used to draw faces in condensation.

events.title: POD EXTERIOR SCAN
events.content:
Hull Temperature: below operational range
Seal Pressure: unstable
Interior Motion: intermittent
Audio Contamination: rising
Manual Override: possible
Warning: signal distortion resembles recursive voice fold
events.caption: Exterior scan of the pod. The pod is physically reachable but unstable.
events.message: [POD EXTERIOR SCAN ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

choices.text: SECURE THE POD BEFORE TOUCHING THE HATCH.

events.text: If it moves, I move with it.
That is both reassuring and horrifying.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: I wish that sentence felt less alive.
Like something inside answers the beacon.
If this goes badly, you picked urgency.
Whole frame is carrying motion through it.
I don't have to like it.
I need to tell you something before you touch the override.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: I have seen this pod before.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: That is such an Ethan answer.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: If you trusted me before, trust me now.
events.speaker: HALE

events.title: OLD POD ACCESS LOG
events.content:
Access Event: Emergency Pod Manual Inspection
Timestamp: Before Incident Alpha
Operator: Ethan Hale
Pod State: empty
Interior Residue: frost / no biological matter detected
Seal Reclosure: completed manually
Current Conflict: active motion and biometrics now detected inside same pod
events.caption: Recovered old access log. Hale previously opened the pod before Alpha.
events.message: [OLD POD ACCESS LOG ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: I hate when a bad answer becomes documented.
And the system learned not to see it.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Whatever is breathing in there is real enough for the pod to keep it alive.

event.speaker: HALE
event.text: Reality is exactly what this station edits best.

event.speaker: MAYA
event.text: Then keep talking while I work.

events.text: Looks old enough to hate me personally.
Hatch clicked twice while I worked.

events.title: POD ACCESS RISK REPORT
events.content:
Interior Motion: detected
Seal Pressure: unstable
Manual Override: required
Audio Channel Contamination: rising
External Voice Traffic: unauthorized
Recommendation: choose access protocol before opening
events.caption: Recovered access risk report. The pod can be opened, but the audio channel is contaminated.
events.message: [POD ACCESS RISK REPORT ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: Fast means I may not control what comes out.
Need to keep the valve steady.
Something hit the inside of the hatch.
I heard it through my gloves.
I can feel it fighting the latch.
Hale is going to remember this.
you commit to whatever is inside.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: you may leave a person to die.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Whatever you choose, I carry it.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Keep your face away from the seam.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: it knows exactly which dead man to wear.
Or the pod is making him look like he is.
I hate you a little right now.

event.speaker: HALE
event.text: Maya, step away from the hatch.

events.text: Need to pull away before the beacon talks me back into this.
That feels like an answer we didn't ask for.
I can hear it clicking through the line.
If we survive the next room, I am going to be angry there too.

directorCut.sourceTitle: THE RESCUE — LIFELINE CUT V6 FINAL
majorChanges: Reduced long waits from 35–72 minutes to 3–4 minutes.
Maya's speech rewritten into short, reactive, physically grounded Lifeline-style messages.
Route choices now produce emotional residue, not only stat changes.
Hale directly references whether the operator trusts him and carries distrust into Episode 10.
Open/abort branch rewritten to create visible guilt, anger and future flags.
Abort branch made much heavier and no longer resolves emotionally in the same scene.
Open branch made more suspenseful by revealing the glove/mark gradually.
Episode 10 chain-effect flags added for every major branch.

episode10UsageNotes.operatorOpenedPod: Maya begins Episode 10 with urgency and fear; Hale questions whether the operator understands what they released.
episode10UsageNotes.operatorAbortedRescue: Maya begins Episode 10 colder and more wounded; Noah/pod beacon guilt remains present in her dialogue.
episode10UsageNotes.mayaRadiationExposure: Maya references suit alarms, metallic taste, and physical strain in later episodes.
episode10UsageNotes.podOpenedUnsafe: Hale directly remembers the forced override and distrusts fast decisions.
episode10UsageNotes.podOpenedSafely: Maya gives the operator more benefit of the doubt during technical rescue steps.
